ruleset.name: Error Prone
ruleset.description: Rules to detect constructs that are either broken, extremely confusing or prone to runtime errors.

rule.name: AvoidBranchingStatementAsLastInLoop
rule.message: Branching statement directly in loop body without conditional guard makes the loop execute only once.
rule.description: Using a `break`, `continue`, or `return` directly in the body of a loop (not guarded by an `if` or `when`) causes the loop to execute at most once. This is almost always a bug or confusing code. Wrap the branching statement inside a conditional to make the intent explicit.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: AvoidCallingFinalize
rule.message: Do not call finalize() explicitly; rely on the garbage collector.
rule.description:
`finalize()` is called by the garbage collector automatically; calling it explicitly bypasses GC timing and can cause objects to be finalized twice. In Kotlin prefer `AutoCloseable` / `use {}` for resource cleanup.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCallingFinalize

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //PostfixUnaryExpression[pmd-kotlin:matchesSig('java.lang.Object#finalize()')] (: it's ok to call super.finalize() from inside an overriding finalize() :) [not(PrimaryExpression/SuperExpression and ancestor::FunctionDeclaration[ SimpleIdentifier/T-Identifier[@Text = 'finalize'] and not(FunctionValueParameters/FunctionValueParameter) ])]

rule.name: AvoidCatchingGenericException
rule.message: Avoid catching overly broad exception types; catch specific exceptions instead.
rule.description:
Catching `Exception` or `RuntimeException` hides real error types and makes error handling imprecise. In Kotlin prefer `runCatching {}` with `.onFailure {}`, or catch specific exceptions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCatchingGenericException

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidCatchingNPE
rule.message: Avoid catching NullPointerException; use Kotlin null-safety (?., ?:, !!) instead.
rule.description:
Catching `NullPointerException` is a code smell in Kotlin. Kotlin's type system distinguishes nullable from non-nullable types at compile time, making explicit NPE catches unnecessary and masking real bugs.
Kotlin idiomatic alternatives: - Use nullable types (`String?`) and safe calls (`str?.length ?: 0`) - Use the Elvis operator (`?:`) for default values - Use `requireNotNull()` or `checkNotNull()` for early validation
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCatchingNPE

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidCatchingThrowable
rule.message: Avoid catching Throwable; it includes Errors that should not be caught.
rule.description:
`Throwable` is the root of both `Exception` and `Error`. Catching it can swallow `OutOfMemoryError`, `StackOverflowError` and other JVM errors that are not recoverable. Catch specific exceptions instead. For result-oriented error handling prefer `runCatching {}` with `.onFailure {}`, which limits handling to non-fatal exceptions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCatchingThrowable

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidDecimalFormatAsField
rule.message: Avoid using DecimalFormat or ChoiceFormat as a field: they are thread-unsafe.
rule.description: `DecimalFormat` and `ChoiceFormat` are thread-unsafe. Declaring them as fields introduces race conditions. Create a new instance per method call, or use a `ThreadLocal`.

rule.name: AvoidDecimalLiteralsInBigDecimalConstructor
rule.message: Avoid creating BigDecimal with a float/double literal; use a String literal instead.
rule.description:
`BigDecimal(0.1)` does not equal `0.1` exactly due to floating-point representation. Use `BigDecimal("0.1")`, `BigDecimal.valueOf(0.1)`, or the Kotlin idiomatic `"0.1".toBigDecimal()` extension for exact decimal values.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidDecimalLiteralsInBigDecimalConstructor

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidRethrowingException
rule.message: Catch block only rethrows the caught exception; remove the try/catch or handle the exception.
rule.description: A catch block that does nothing but rethrow the caught exception is pointless. Remove the `try/catch` entirely, or handle the exception in a meaningful way (logging, wrapping, recovery).

property.name: xpath

rule.name: AvoidThrowingNullPointerException
rule.message: Avoid throwing NullPointerException; use requireNotNull() or checkNotNull() instead.
rule.description: Explicitly throwing `NullPointerException` is misleading -- callers and tools expect NPE to indicate an unintentional null dereference, not a deliberate contract violation. Use `requireNotNull()`, `checkNotNull()`, or `error()` to signal intentional null checks with a clear message.

rule.name: AvoidThrowingRawExceptionTypes
rule.message: Avoid throwing raw exception types; use a more specific exception subclass.
rule.description: Throwing generic root types (`Throwable`, `Error`, `Exception`, `RuntimeException`) loses semantic information. Callers cannot catch the specific condition without catching everything. Use a specific subclass that communicates the failure reason, or define a custom exception.

rule.name: DoNotExtendJavaLangError
rule.message: Do not extend Error or its standard subclasses; extend Exception or RuntimeException instead.
rule.description:
`java.lang.Error` and its subclasses (`AssertionError`, `LinkageError`, `VirtualMachineError`, `ThreadDeath`) represent serious JVM-level problems that application code should not model. Extending them creates custom error types that callers will not catch, since `catch(e: Exception)` does not catch `Error`.
Use `Exception` or `RuntimeException` (or a Kotlin sealed class hierarchy) instead.

rule.name: DoNotTerminateVM
rule.message: Avoid calling System.exit() or Runtime.halt(); throw an exception or use a proper shutdown hook instead.
rule.description:
Calling `System.exit()` or `Runtime.halt()` terminates the JVM abruptly, bypassing shutdown hooks, resource cleanup, and normal exception handling. In library code this is especially harmful as it kills the entire host process.
Prefer throwing an appropriate exception, or for application entry points register a shutdown hook via `Runtime.addShutdownHook()` and signal shutdown through a flag or coroutine cancellation.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/DoNotTerminateVM

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: EqualsNull
rule.message: Use == null or != null instead of .equals(null)
rule.description: Tests for null should not use the `equals()` method. The `==` operator should be used instead. In Kotlin, `==` is null-safe and more idiomatic than calling `obj.equals(null)`.

rule.name: OverrideBothEqualsAndHashcode
rule.message: Ensure you override both equals() and hashCode()
rule.description:
Override both `equals(other: Any?)` and `hashCode()`, or override neither. Even if you are inheriting a `hashCode()` from a parent class, consider implementing it explicitly and delegating to the superclass.
The idiomatic Kotlin solution is to use a `data class`, which generates correct `equals()` and `hashCode()` automatically based on the primary constructor properties.

rule.name: ReplaceJavaUtilDate
rule.message: Avoid using java.util.Date; use java.time API instead
rule.description: The legacy `java.util.Date` class is mutable, not thread-safe, and has a confusing API. Use the `java.time` API instead: `LocalDate` (date only), `LocalTime` (time only), `LocalDateTime` (date and time), `Instant` (timestamp), or `ZonedDateTime` (with timezone).

rule.name: ReturnEmptyCollectionRatherThanNull
rule.message: Return an empty collection rather than null from a Collection/Map function
rule.description:
Returning `null` from a function that has a nullable `Collection` or `Map` return type forces callers to null-check the result before using it. Returning an empty collection (`emptyList()`, `emptyMap()`, `emptySet()`) is less error-prone and leads to cleaner call sites.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/ReturnEmptyCollectionRatherThanNull

property.name: xpath
property.value: //JumpExpression[ T-RETURN and Expression[pmd-kotlin:nodeText() = 'null'] and not(ancestor::LambdaLiteral) and not(ancestor::AnonymousFunction) and ancestor::FunctionDeclaration[1][ pmd-kotlin:isNullable() and (pmd-kotlin:typeIs('java.util.Collection') or pmd-kotlin:typeIs('java.util.Map') or pmd-kotlin:typeIs('kotlin.Array')) ] ] | //FunctionDeclaration[ pmd-kotlin:isNullable() and FunctionBody/Expression[pmd-kotlin:nodeText() = 'null'] and (pmd-kotlin:typeIs('java.util.Collection') or pmd-kotlin:typeIs('java.util.Map') or pmd-kotlin:typeIs('kotlin.Array')) ]

rule.name: ReturnFromFinallyBlock
rule.message: Avoid returning from a finally block; this can discard exceptions
rule.description: Avoid returning from a `finally` block. Returning from a `finally` block silently discards any exception that was thrown in the corresponding `try` or `catch` block, making bugs very hard to find.

rule.name: SimpleDateFormatNeedsLocale
rule.message: Specify a Locale when constructing a SimpleDateFormat
rule.description: Be sure to specify a `Locale` when creating `SimpleDateFormat` instances to ensure that locale-appropriate formatting is used. Without a locale, the default system locale is used which can cause unexpected results in internationalized applications. Prefer `java.time` APIs (such as `DateTimeFormatter`) which have explicit locale support.

rule.name: UnnecessaryCaseChange
rule.message: Unnecessary case change before equals(); use equals with ignoreCase = true instead
rule.description: Using `toLowerCase()` or `toUpperCase()` just to do a case-insensitive comparison is wasteful. In Kotlin, use `equals(other, ignoreCase = true)` for strings, or `equalsIgnoreCase()` from the Java API. This avoids creating a temporary String object.

rule.name: UnresolvedType
rule.message: Type ''{0}'' could not be resolved -- add the missing dependency to the PMD analysis classpath for full type-aware analysis.
rule.description:
Reports import declarations whose type could not be resolved by the `kotlin-type-mapper` pre-analysis step. This typically means that a library or SDK dependency is not on the PMD analysis classpath.
When types are unresolved, type-aware rules (such as `UseStringBuilderForStringAppends`, `UseLocaleWithCaseConversions`, and `ReplaceJavaUtilDate`) may miss violations or report false positives for code that uses those types.
To fix: pass the missing JARs via the PMD `--classpath` option (or the `auxclasspath` configuration in your build plugin) so the type mapper can resolve all referenced types.

rule.name: UseLocaleWithCaseConversions
rule.message: String.toLowerCase()/toUpperCase() use the default system Locale; pass an explicit Locale or use Kotlin's lowercase()/uppercase().
rule.description:
Calling `java.lang.String#toLowerCase()` or `#toUpperCase()` without a `Locale` argument uses the default system `Locale`, which can produce unexpected results for locale-sensitive characters (e.g., Turkish `'i'` -> `'I'` with `toUpperCase(Locale("tr"))`).
Kotlin idiomatic alternatives: - `str.lowercase()` / `str.uppercase()` -- use `Locale.ROOT` (locale-insensitive, safe for identifiers, file names, protocol keywords) - `str.lowercase(Locale.getDefault())` / `str.uppercase(Locale.getDefault())` -- explicit locale-sensitive conversion for user-visible strings
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/UseLocaleWithCaseConversions

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.
